review_text: QUIET BUT NOT REALLY FAMILY FRIENDLY NO FOOD ETC EXCEPT FOR OAK RESTAURANT AT SHELL SERVICE STATION ACROSS ROAD WHICH CLOSES AT 10PM USUALLY BUT SOMETIMES EARLIER MENS & WOMAN'S AMENITIES BOTH HAVE BORE WATER NOT REALLY THE PLACE FOR A FAMILY TO STAY UNLESS YOU ARE SO TIRED OR HAVE CAR TROUBLE MYSELF I MAY STAY 1 NIGHT IF I HAD FAMILY I WOULD BYPASS TO CESSNOCK CARAVAN PARK PHOTOS NOT WORTH TAKING SORRY BUT IT IS WHAT IT IS BUT LET ME SAY THIS IF YOU TAKE DRUGS ETC THEN THIS IS THE PLACE FOR YOU AS YOU WILL FIT RIGHT IN FIT GET IT LOL

With the cost of living through the roof FSCP is a handy stop gap measure until you can get back on your feet. The residents are genuine battlers who are just trying to get by. Provides a niche service to those in need.

Absolutely disgusting and unsafe place to live or have children. The park is heavily infested with cockroaches, and the bathrooms are often dirty and poorly maintained.

There is also a camera positioned facing the female toilets, which is extremely concerning and uncomfortable for residents.

The caretaker, Glen, regularly patrols the park wearing what appears to be a security-style uniform. Residents have expressed concerns about his behaviour, including walking around late at night and looking into caravan windows. Many people in the park feel uncomfortable and unsafe because of this.

Quiet hours are strictly enforced from 10pm, and residents feel intimidated about making any noise after this time.

Overall, this place does not feel safe or well managed. These concerns need to be taken seriously and looked into by the appropriate authorities.

Hi...
My cousin lives at the park. It's a nice family park. She says they feel safe there. Glen the groundsman is good with kids, he let's them be kids and makes sure my cousin and her kids don't need anything. So thank you.

The bathroom can be cleaned better. Very slippery floor when showers are on and mould on walls plus spider webs everywhere ha ha

Recently there has been some reviews about the caravan park and some of the staff that are totally not true. My partner and I have lived at the caravan park since January 2026 and we are very happy living here. The park is a safe place to live for both adults and children and a great place to relax and enjoy life. We have found the staff to be friendly, helpful and very respectful and the same goes for the other people that live here. The caravan park is quiet and peaceful and I feel completely safe walking to the toilet by myself at night.

I’ve lived here twice in the past few years. The second time I lived here I saw an incredible difference in the park. The community is safe and family oriented, supportive with services and looks amazing. Glenn helps everything run smoothly and is always on top of any problem that might come about. Would recommend to anyone who wants somewhere safe and reliable.
I'm happy here as it's up to you to keep it clean. It's super quite at night and for the $$$ it's value for the money.

Glen the park manager is a control freak extremely over bearing and makes passes at every one. The permanent residents don't like him but have no choice because they have no where else to go and he knows it he definitely  imposes and likes to involve him self even likes to take a seat and join in . The electricity payment situation is rediclous the bore water  smells like sewerage. Glen definitely gives off vibes of that tv series " trailer Park boys"  it has a very uncomfortable vibe  especially when  he walks around the park patrolling like he has a badge. And when it turns 10pm evey one knows it's bed time better not make a sound , but be sured he will let you know it's ten pm even if it means a knock on your door some times he doesn't even think he needs to knock will just come on in.. ther are definitely some people at the park you want to keep your kids away from . .

There were 3 of us travelling from Queensland to Melbourne and had been on the road for a few hours, tired and sweaty as we had driven from extreme heat we needed to stop for the day. Freeman’s Springs Waterhole caravan park popped into view.
We stopped and rang phone number on office office wall and spoke to Paula who advised we wld be greeted by Glen.
Not 5 seconds later Glen appeared advised us where to park for the night as we had a Toyota Coaster and a small wagon … $65.00 later we are settled in safe and secure
Did our laundry via a top loader which is provided for free (bring your own washing powder/liquid) and we also used the dryer which was $6 for 1 hour & 30 mins 👍
Shower/toilet facilities are clean
Glen could not have been friendlier, accomodating and just a genuinely nice bloke, if we are ever passing that way again at any time we will Definately be stopping there again
Kids at the park where having fun

These people need financial help, having to live in a shipping container would be horrible, the people there were polite when I visited. I didn't stay but it definitely needs assistance in the upgrade department... Unfortunately society under communism favours the greedy and the corporate and fairness and justice of hardworking Australians is being eroded at its core foundation... Please help these people... I gave 5 stars because they showed me kindness every time I visited...

I haven't stayed there but it looks like a Clayton's drink, the drink you have when you not having a drink.... Clayton Stevenson left a one star review his visits sounded a lot like this place when I first turned up...and i had very old van that was on par with your description Clayton.... there's a lovely blue house next door to me would make a lovely new police station just needs finishing touches almost finished bathroom for the boys if they looking for a new station... ya won't find any horses on this proposed station like Clayton's tv ad, the Freeman's caravan park horses up da mountain but put your bid in,and ya best offer forward you might just be lucky with God of good luck 🤞🤞 this is da vision of the new rainbow sheriff's house with the stars on the roof like America sheriff badge...you might wanna roll ya sleeves up when ya paintin the big dog emblem at da front so ya don't get rainbow paint all over ya...God loves a good rainbow with the rainbow painted dog you would be  more  bright, colourful and welcoming.. star on the roof matching sheriff badge gifts for all the kiddies . ⭐👮💃🕺I got bee gees  cd I play staying alive if ya like some dancing while ya paintin....😉

Disgusting cochroach infested slum. Full of drugos and deadbeats. Water is contaminated. Constant smell of sewerage. Owners are liars. Promise the world but give you nothing. I had to supply my own beds as the van did not even have beds.
Dirty bathrooms. vans & annexes were a mess. Staff were rude & imposing on visitors.
